path: laptop speaker -> quiet room (~0.3 m, occasional car) -> iPhone Continuity mic
capture_clock_note: sent symbol of duration T appears as ~0.88*T in the 48kHz-labelled iPhone recording; a modem MUST correct ~12% clock error (codec's tape-speed search only spans +-6%).
freq_response_note: channel passes ~400 Hz-10 kHz within ~11 dB; both FSK tones present and ~2 dB apart -> spectrally fine.
verdict: 2-FSK reliably carried up to ~50 baud; clean with margin <=40 baud; hard cliff ~60-67 baud (reverb/ISI). Real CAS3 codec is 1200 baud -> ~24x too fast for this acoustic path.
context: Digital line-in capture impossible on this MacBook Air (3.5mm jack is mic-port only). Acoustic capture via iPhone is the fallback; viable only for a slow (~40 baud) modem.
